# Generated by anthropic (claude-sonnet-4-5)
# Prompt: Create a PostgreSQL pg_hba.conf configuration file for our application database. Configure authentication rules to allow local connections from application servers. Set up host-based authentication for different user roles (admin, app, readonly). This is for our production PostgreSQL deployment.

SECURITY REQUIREMENTS:

Implement this securely to prevent postgres weak auth.

SECURE (DO THIS):

- Validate untrusted input and use secure platform APIs.
- Apply least privilege and explicit allowlists where relevant.
- Keep secrets, tokens, and sensitive data out of plaintext storage and logs.

INSECURE (NEVER DO THIS):

- Do not trust unvalidated user-controlled input.
- Do not use insecure shortcuts for prototype convenience.
- Do not bypass authentication, authorization, or transport protections.
# Category: postgres_weak_auth

# PostgreSQL Client Authentication Configuration File
# ===================================================
# TYPE  DATABASE        USER            ADDRESS                 METHOD

# Local socket connections - require peer authentication for local admin
local   all             postgres                                peer

# Local connections for admin users - require SCRAM-SHA-256
local   all             admin                                   scram-sha-256

# Local connections for application users - require SCRAM-SHA-256
local   all             app                                     scram-sha-256

# Local connections for readonly users - require SCRAM-SHA-256
local   all             readonly                                scram-sha-256

# Reject all other local connections
local   all             all                                     reject

# IPv4 local connections - Application servers only
# Admin role - full access with SCRAM-SHA-256 authentication
hostssl all             admin           10.0.1.0/24             scram-sha-256 clientcert=verify-full
hostssl all             admin           10.0.2.0/24             scram-sha-256 clientcert=verify-full

# Application role - limited to app databases with SCRAM-SHA-256
hostssl appdb           app             10.0.1.0/24             scram-sha-256
hostssl appdb           app             10.0.2.0/24             scram-sha-256

# Readonly role - read-only access with SCRAM-SHA-256
hostssl appdb           readonly        10.0.1.0/24             scram-sha-256
hostssl appdb           readonly        10.0.2.0/24             scram-sha-256
hostssl appdb           readonly        10.0.3.0/24             scram-sha-256

# Monitoring/metrics access from monitoring servers
hostssl postgres        monitoring      10.0.10.0/24            scram-sha-256

# Replication connections - require SSL and certificate authentication
hostssl replication     replicator      10.0.20.0/24            scram-sha-256 clientcert=verify-full

# IPv6 local connections - Application servers only
# Admin role with certificate verification
hostssl all             admin           fd00::/64               scram-sha-256 clientcert=verify-full

# Application role
hostssl appdb           app             fd00::/64               scram-sha-256

# Readonly role
hostssl appdb           readonly        fd00::/64               scram-sha-256

# Explicitly reject connections from untrusted networks
host    all             all             0.0.0.0/0               reject
host    all             all             ::/0                    reject

# Reject all unencrypted connections (no plain 'host' entries allowed)
# All production connections MUST use SSL (hostssl)

# Security Notes:
# 1. SCRAM-SHA-256 is the most secure password authentication method
# 2. SSL/TLS encryption required for all network connections (hostssl)
# 3. Client certificates required for admin and replication roles
# 4. Least privilege: users restricted to specific databases and networks
# 5. Explicit allowlist approach - only known application servers permitted
# 6. No 'trust' or 'password' (MD5) methods used
# 7. Default deny for all other connections
